bindings.question_text: What does this number actually mean?
bindings.answer_text: A Q&A layout is useful when the post needs to explain one point clearly. The question sets up the reader, and the answer gives the short explanation underneath.
bindings.bottom_note: Use this for explainers, definitions, or myth-versus-fact style posts.
bindings.visual_1_caption: First visual caption or short explanation
bindings.visual_2_caption: Second visual caption or comparison point
bindings.body_text: This layout gives the visual most of the slide, then adds a short explanation underneath. It works well for a chart, photo, or map with context.
bindings.timeline_intro: Use this layout for a clean sequence of key events or milestones.
bindings.event_1_body: A short explanation of the first point in the sequence.
bindings.event_2_body: Useful context that explains what changed next.
bindings.event_3_body: Another key moment, result, decision, or update.
bindings.event_4_body: A concise ending point or current status.
bindings.ranking_intro: Compact layout for ordered top-ten results.
bindings.ranking_text:
1. Highest participation — 92%
2. Second result — 87%
3. Third result — 81%
4. Fourth result — 76%
5. Fifth result — 71%
6. Sixth result — 68%
7. Seventh result — 64%
8. Eighth result — 61%
9. Ninth result — 58%
10. Tenth result — 54%
bindings.quote_text: A clear quote can carry the whole slide when the surrounding design stays quiet.
bindings.left_body:
Lower participation
Less visible trend
Harder to compare
bindings.right_body:
Higher participation
Clearer trend
Easy side-by-side read
bindings.footer_text: Template preview only | Eirepolitic data pipeline
